sections.paragraph:
δειλινός, ή, όν (Diocles 141 p. 180, 12; Strabo, Plut., Lucian, pap, LXX) in the afternoon τὸ δειλινόν as adv. toward evening (Menand., Kon. 7; Lucian, Lex. 2; Gen 3:8) Ac 3:1 D (d: ad vesperum).—DELG s.v. δείελος.

Diocles Diocles of Carystus, med., IVb.c.—List 5

Strabo Strabo , I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Plut Plut , I–II a.d.—List 5

Lucian Lucian , II a.d.—List 5

pap pap = papyrus, -yri

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

adv. adv. = adverb, adverbially

Menand Menand , various dramas cited; IV–III b.c.—List 5

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 215
